name: foundationdb
explanation: FoundationDB clients connect through a binary cluster file (`fdb.cluster`) which contains a description, an ID, and the coordinator IP/port list. The check uses the `fdb` Python binding to call `fdb.open(cluster_file=...)` and then reads the `\xff\xff/status/json` special key. There is no host/port to probe directly; without the cluster file there is no way for the discovery layer to address the database. TLS-secured deployments additionally require a key/cert/CA bundle. The cluster file is host-specific state (typically provisioned by the FoundationDB installer or operator) and cannot be derived over the wire.
notes: Spec marks `cluster_file` as optional because the binding will fall back to standard locations (e.g. `/etc/foundationdb/fdb.cluster`) if the file exists there. That still means the credential-equivalent (the cluster file) must already be present on the Agent host.
